text:
There once was a girl named Lily who loved the moon. So much that she sleep sleep slept just listening to its quiet music. Whenever she listened to a favorite old record, she imagined the moonlit nights.

One night, after hearing a record she'd listened to countless times, she dreamed that the moon danced just for her. She awoke feeling giggly and whispered to it, "I love you. Just a little."

The moon blinked twice and whispered back, "Love you too."

From that day on, whenever she looked up at the stars, Lily imagined they were smiling back at her.

Every night was like that—an ode to a distant dance. Until one dusk, as she lay listening, something swooped silently through the sky. With a golden glow, it fluttered down beside her.

It was just an old hawk,/owl couple they'd saved. They spoke with owl feathers brushing against Lily's cheek.
